# Integrate Google OAuth Authentication

## Description
Add Google OAuth authentication to allow users to sign in with their Google accounts, providing a seamless login experience and enabling Google Calendar integration.

## Use Case
Users can click "Sign in with Google" and authenticate using their Google account, which will also enable future Google Calendar sync features.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Google OAuth login button
- [ ] Secure token handling and storage
- [ ] User profile creation from Google data
- [ ] Integration with existing auth system
- [ ] Proper error handling for auth failures
- [ ] Logout functionality
- [ ] Account linking for existing users

## Technical Requirements
- [ ] Set up Google OAuth 2.0 credentials
- [ ] Implement OAuth flow with Supabase Auth
- [ ] Add Google login UI component
- [ ] Handle user profile data from Google
- [ ] Update auth context for Google users
- [ ] Add proper error handling and fallbacks

## Implementation Notes
- Use Supabase Auth with Google provider
- Store Google user data in profiles table
- Consider email verification flow
- Handle existing users with same email
